items.prompt: Solve `x + 7 = 19` and show how each step preserves equality.
items.expected_evidence: evaluate bounded rational-number expressions while preserving operation and grouping structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates evaluate bounded rational-number expressions while preserving operation and grouping structure with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Solve `4x = 28`, then check the solution by substitution.
items.expected_evidence: interpret absolute value on a number line as distance from zero
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates interpret absolute value on a number line as distance from zero with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Match the equation `n - 5 = 12` to a balance or missing-value situation and solve it.
items.expected_evidence: distinguish value, opposite, and absolute value in bounded cases
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates distinguish value, opposite, and absolute value in bounded cases with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain why subtracting the same value from both sides of an equation preserves the solution set.
items.expected_evidence: evaluate bounded rational-number expressions while preserving operation and grouping structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to evaluate bounded rational-number expressions while preserving operation and grouping structure.

items.prompt: Explain what the variable represents and why inverse operations help isolate it.
items.expected_evidence: interpret absolute value on a number line as distance from zero
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to interpret absolute value on a number line as distance from zero.

items.prompt: A student solves `x + 5 = 13` by writing `x = 13 + 5`. Diagnose the first structural mistake. Focus on structure tracking breakdown.
items.expected_evidence: evaluate bounded rational-number expressions while preserving operation and grouping structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward evaluate bounded rational-number expressions while preserving operation and grouping structure.

items.prompt: A student solves `3x = 18` by dividing only the `x`. Explain what went wrong. Focus on absolute value as sign removal.
items.expected_evidence: interpret absolute value on a number line as distance from zero
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward interpret absolute value on a number line as distance from zero.

items.prompt: A gym charges a $12 signup fee plus $5 per class. Write and solve an equation for the number of classes if the total was $32.
items.expected_evidence: evaluate bounded rational-number expressions while preserving operation and grouping structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves evaluate bounded rational-number expressions while preserving operation and grouping structure in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: A balance model has three equal bags and 6 loose counters on one side and 24 counters on the other. Write and solve the equation.
items.expected_evidence: interpret absolute value on a number line as distance from zero
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves interpret absolute value on a number line as distance from zero in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Two weeks later, solve `m/3 = 8` and explain how you know the result is reasonable.
items.expected_evidence: evaluate bounded rational-number expressions while preserving operation and grouping structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates evaluate bounded rational-number expressions while preserving operation and grouping structure on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Before solving numerical expressions and absolute value, complete a prerequisite check on signed rational number fluency and order and explain how it supports the current equation work.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether signed rational number fluency and order is secure enough to support numerical expressions and absolute value
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in signed rational number fluency and order from a current-node issue in numerical expressions and absolute value.
items.suspected_knowledge_gap_if_incorrect: signed rational number fluency and order

items.prompt: Use a quick variable expressions and substitution check to explain whether the learner is blocked by prerequisite structure or by numerical expressions and absolute value itself.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether variable expressions and substitution is secure enough to support numerical expressions and absolute value
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in variable expressions and substitution from a current-node issue in numerical expressions and absolute value.
